acceptance_targets: [chapter_02_craft_001] PASS if the revised passage anchored to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:217 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:313 shows Ida's emotional state in-scene (not only via Jalen report) and includes at least one direct line where Lena acknowledges the harm from taking the key; FAIL if Ida's crying remains purely second-hand exposition.

[chapter_02_prose_001] PASS if, across the four cited lines, no more than two retain the exact "smell(ed) like" construction and the replacements provide concrete non-olfactory perception; FAIL if three or more of those lines still use the same formula.

[chapter_02_prose_002] PASS if the revised text at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:245 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:271 removes colon-labeled fragment lists and verbless inventory strings, replacing them with complete sentences that still clearly mark what is present and missing; FAIL if fragment-list syntax remains the primary construction in those beats.

[FBR-02-009] Pass if chapter_02 contains only this one retained impact-simile template and no second near-identical template in the same scene; fail if additional 'landed/settled ... like ...' lines remain in chapter_02.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passage anchored to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:217 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:313 shows Ida's emotional state in-scene (not only via Jalen report) and includes at least one direct line where Lena acknowledges the harm from taking the key; FAIL if Ida's crying remains purely second-hand exposition.
findings.problem: Sanitization/tension defusal at a peak family-consequence beat: after Lena chooses the case over Ida in the pantry rupture, Ida's emotional break is reported second-hand by Jalen instead of being rendered on-page, which softens the immediate human cost of Lena's choice.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the consequence bridge anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:217 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:313. Add an on-page aftermath beat (immediately after :217 or during the callback sequence at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:317 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:325) where Lena directly encounters and responds to Ida's hurt, with specific bodily/social consequence rather than relay summary.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, across the four cited lines, no more than two retain the exact "smell(ed) like" construction and the replacements provide concrete non-olfactory perception; FAIL if three or more of those lines still use the same formula.
findings.problem: Recurring prose formula ("smelled like ...") appears four times and begins to read as autopilot sensory scaffolding rather than intentional variation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework at least two of the repeated constructions at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:9, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:27, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:133, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:305 so the sensory setup varies by syntax and channel (sound, touch, spatial pressure) while staying in Lena-close perception.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised text at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:245 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:271 removes colon-labeled fragment lists and verbless inventory strings, replacing them with complete sentences that still clearly mark what is present and missing; FAIL if fragment-list syntax remains the primary construction in those beats.
findings.problem: Elliptical clipping in the storage-inventory reveal (fragment chains like "Second box: ... Third box: ..." and "No red twine, no ledger...") pushes narration toward telegraphic shorthand and weakens sentence-level control at a key discovery moment.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the inventory and absence beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:245 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:271 into fully grammatical sentences with explicit subject-verb structure, while preserving urgency through cadence rather than fragment clipping.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if chapter_02 contains only this one retained impact-simile template and no second near-identical template in the same scene; fail if additional 'landed/settled ... like ...' lines remain in chapter_02.
findings.problem: Recurring full-book formula metaphor pattern: 'landed/settled/felt ... like ...' appears across 6 chapters (chapter_02, chapter_08, chapter_10, chapter_13, chapter_16, chapter_18). KEEP this chapter's line as one of at most two retained instances.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:532 as an anchor, but avoid adding parallel 'landed/settled ... like ...' constructions nearby in the same emotional beat at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:534 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:540.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When edge cases arise, choose the version that preserves uncomfortable specificity and character accountability; do not replace difficult scenes with procedural summary, and do not mute class, race, sexuality, or institutional cruelty when those forces are actively shaping choices.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene with violence, racism, grief, or moral compromise is rendered in abstract or polite shorthand, revise to restore specific bodily, social, and material consequence before accepting the draft.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the baseline in contemporary Mississippi speech; uncontracted forms are reserved for formality, emphasis, or social performance.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: rotate structural textures across chapters: procedural surge, quiet domestic interval, document-driven slow burn, dread preparation, aftermath inventory, and information-turn chapters; avoid repeating scene count plus dialogue density pattern in adjacent chapters.
prose_style_profile.diction: literary but grounded; precise legal and forensic terms when needed, balanced by lived vernacular and local material detail.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person anchored primarily to Lena, with brief controlled expansions only when factual clarity requires it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: alternate compressed procedural sentences with longer sensory-emotional lines so pressure and reflection keep rebalancing.
